All right, now we are jumping into the next part of retention, which is attendance reports each Wednesday evening. So we want to make sure that we do it on Wednesday evenings. That gives them two to three chances to get into the gym before it's probably pretty likely they're not going to come in that week. Like if they miss the first one, two, three days of the week, they probably aren't going to make it the rest of the week. And if that trend continues, that may be a big reason that they exit is they're not getting results because they're not coming in, they're not finding the value in it. So we need to make sure that they are coming in on a regular basis, but we need to be proactive about it, which is why we are tracking attendance and then sending them a message to let them know that we are totally watching and holding them accountable and making sure that they get their workouts in. Also reach out to anyone that has not been into the gym that week. So we're going to show you how to do that. We're going to try to include some humor and accountability. So it's not like super strict like, where are you? You know, it's it's more trying to be funny, but also let them know that you're serious, you know. And then finally, we're going to show you how to record this on the members tracking sheet. So at any time, anybody can look and see about what your attendance looks like across your membership base. So with that being said, we are going to jump into how to generate a attendance report. I'm going to show you guys in mind body because that's the CRM that I'm using, but it should be pretty similar depending on which CRM really we're just trying to show the process of actually getting the report and transferring it from your CRM to the member tracking sheet. So let's dig into that now. All right, guys. So now we are going to dig into generating a report and attendance report in your CRM and then transferring that information over to your tracking sheets for your members. That way you can identify who needs to get a message and who doesn't need to get a message and your owner or whoever that just wants to pop in and view it can see it really quickly if you're if they're not the person that's actually sending the messages or generated reports. So to do this in mind body, I'm assuming it's similar in other CRMs. The point is you should be able to generate a report based on attendance. So I'm going to click on reports and I am going to go into clients and then I am going to go into last visit. So I'm trying to identify when their last visit was at the gym. So if I know it's within a certain amount of date that they've been in anybody else, they haven't been to the gym and I need a message. So like I said, it's going to be the last three days. So we'll click on today is May 21. So I'm going to target from 19 to 21st. Typically you want to do it on Wednesdays because that way Wednesday evenings, that gives them a chance to come in Wednesday, Thursday, I'm sorry, Monday, Tuesday or Wednesday. Or Sundays if you start on Sundays, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday. The point is that if they haven't been to the gym by Wednesday evening of the week, they're probably not going to come in. And when people stop coming in that week, it might lead to two weeks and three weeks and all of a sudden they're not using their gym membership and they cancel. So this is why it's part of retention to help them not cancel by being proactive and reaching out to them as soon as you notice that they're not utilizing what they're paying for. And that is amazing for accountability and they're going to recognize that. They're like, man, my trainer is going to message me if I don't get to the gym by Wednesday. That's awesome. That's huge accountability. And that's exactly what we're doing. So someone that's had one or more visits before the 21st, we click go. It's now going to generate a report for us that shows all of our members who have been to the gym at least one time in the past few days. So here we go. And they won't be very high numbers like one, three, one, one, two, one. Okay. So it's just a list of all the members that have been in at least once. And now we can take this list and we can cross-reference it with our master tracking list for members and we can start utilizing that. So I'm going to, you can print this off. I like to just keep it open and bounce back and forth between screens, but it's an alphabetical order and your other sheet should be in alphabetical order as well. So this is, again, we just went over this, but the members tracking sheets and it's broken up between trainer A, B, C or D. Now for this purpose, what I like to do is I like to create a copy. So I'm just going to duplicate it and I'm going to name this, I'm going to rename it and I'm going to call it attendance. And the reason I'm going to do that is because typically your trainers are not going to be doing the attendance report. It's more of like an admin role. And so you want like either the owner or the retention manager or somebody else to generate the attendance report and send out all the messages or even at least send them out on behalf of the trainers. Otherwise it's going to take them a lot of time to do it. So you should be able to send it out like on behalf of the trainers or even just give them a list of people that they, they need a text that haven't been in yet. So here this is the one I named attendance and all I'm going to do is grab the names from the other sheets. So I made a copy of trainer D. So now I'm going to grab these. So copy and go everybody on the exact same sheet. I'll grab B under attendance and I'm going to paste that in there. And then finally I'll go to trainer A. And then when you put a character in there, they'll turn, they'll turn green. So let me just put the, we'll take a look at the sheet here. So as an example, Edmira, she's probably not on my sample, but the point is you're going to want to go from one sheet over to the next sheet and simply just put in how many visits they had, right? Two, and it'll start populating your sheet with different colors. Let's say this person didn't have any visits, you just skip it over. Three, this person had no visits. One, and that person had none. So as an example, we have like two people that did not show up. So those are the people that we need to message. You're not going to message anybody that came in because they're doing a great job and I think it's just reaching out too much and they might not appreciate it. So what you can do now is you can sort this and it has all of the people that visited up top, right? So now we know that we want to start here and work our way down. These would be everybody that did a visit. You'll have all of your names on here. You would technically, I'm using this one as an example, but these are the people that we want to message. Harrison, Nora, and Venus. Those aren't actual members of mine, but I'm going to show you as an example how you could do this. There's two different ways you can message these people. A freeway would be in Boxer. So if you're using the Boxer app, you can just send them a message one at a time and it'll probably take you, I don't know, maybe 30 minutes to text maybe 50 to 60 people just with the same message copy and paste. Okay, how's it going? I prefer to use like Scipio because then you can input their first name. So I'll show you how I would do it on Scipio. So you would just jump into Scipio. You would click on add a new message or create a new message. So what it's going to do now is it's going to pick up a, it's going to open up a new message and here is where you can type in all the people at once that you want to message. So you could say if Bob was somebody, Bob's a name, maybe Jessica, Jesse, Greg. Okay, so you have all the people in there that you need to send a message to. You would click on now. So we want to send it right away or if you want to delay it or whatever. And then you would type in the body. So it would say something like, hey, and then you'll use the brackets so you can do first name, contacts, first name. Hey, first name. Just noticed you haven't made it in this week yet. We really miss you. And then you can put an emoji of maybe somebody that is crying or something. Is your next workout going to be so pretty simple and it just, it gets right to the point. It's like, Hey, you know, first name, just notice you haven't made it to the week or made it in this week yet. We really miss you. What's up? When's your next workout going to be? So that is something that you could do. And then I would change it every week. Just in case there's multiple week offenders, I would change it in, change it up every week. And I think that would be something that would not get people frustrated or angry, but they totally know that you're just checking up with them. So you can keep track of this and make sure that you are being consistent because if they notice that you are messaging them one week, but not the next week, that consistency will not make them think that you're always on top of it. So then you would just hit continue and send. If you wanted to send it out to all these people at once, it's a little bit quicker than messaging them all from like Boxer or a cell phone or something, but it's either way works. Okay. And then the only thing I will note as like a personal note is if there is, I'm going to leave this. If for whatever reason they, their Wednesday, just put that in the notes. So you could have it under this person and say, no attendance message. Trains Saturday. If you have somebody like that, right? Just put it in the notes. So then you know when you're looking at it to not send them a message or you can run a attendance report just for that person or people that fit a different bill and see if they're still coming in and send them like a specific one, but Monday through Wednesday should cover like 95% of your entire membership base. So that is exactly how you want to do this. It might take an hour, maybe two hours when you first get used to it per week, but that compared to the, even if it boosts, if it moves your attrition down 1%, think about how much money you're making in that hour or two hours, right? And it's also something that is very easily delegatable. That's a word. You can teach your front desk or your admin or you can teach this just under or your Scipio. I mean, they'll send the message on your behalf. And then they just reply to responses. You want them to do this, you could easily show them how to do it. And but I think it makes a little bit more sense to have an admin do this because it is highly administrative and maybe they can send the message to the trainers clients on behalf of the trainer via Vox trainer setup. So that is what I wanted to show you for attendance tracking. It's really important to do. This is in systems that's completely fine. The point is that you should be tracking attendance of your members by every Wednesday to see if they came in that week. And if they have not come in, send them a message.
